title: helianthus excubitor
intro: Helianthus excubitor, commonly known as the false sunflower, is a plant species in the daisy family, characterized by its showy yellow flowers and hairy leaves. The plant's roots and leaves have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in the treatment of various health issues. Some of the health benefits associated with Helianthus excubitor include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive problems, and act as an antiseptic to prevent infection. Helianthus excubitor can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
